content.claim: Six- and eight-year gimnázium programs are geographically concentrated in large urban settlements and Budapest, with historically established and larger schools retaining greater selective capacity than later-founded or rural schools regardless of track type, indicating path-dependent geographic stratification.
content.context: Fresh live research through the School network planning lens
limitations: Geographic mapping data is from 1999/2000; current distribution may have shifted
Selective capacity correlation is documented but causation is not explicitly tested
Does not quantify the actual travel distance/time burden for rural applicants seeking selective tracks
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
